target_game.name: Bounce Blast 3D
target_game.core_loop: Players tap to select and place colored blocks from a tray into a main grid to clear obstacles, which then transform into bouncing balls that trigger physics-based chain reactions to clear the level.
target_game.audience_proxy: Casual puzzle players, aged 25-55, who enjoy satisfying physics-based games as a relaxing time-filler.

palette.description: A dark, moody background with deep purple UI frames and a bright yellow-orange accent for progress bars and highlights.

top_archetypes.label: Satisfaction · live-action-UGC

centroid_hook.visual_action: A young man waves his arms above his head and dances to a beat in a dimly lit room.
centroid_hook.text_overlay: how I feel after playing Crossword Go all night after school

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 10d. Hook representative: "A young man dances energetically in a blue-lit room, expressing his happiness af"

top_archetypes.rationale: 2 creatives across 2 unique advertisers, average age 94d. Hook representative: "A user interacts with a digital jigsaw puzzle on their phone, dragging pieces in"

top_archetypes.label: Satisfaction · 3D-render

centroid_hook.summary: A drawer opens in a kitchen, and then red pepper is satisfyingly poured into a glass jar.
centroid_hook.visual_action: A pink kitchen drawer opens, and then red pepper flakes are poured into a clear jar labeled "RED PEPPER".

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 143d. Hook representative: "A drawer opens in a kitchen, and then red pepper is satisfyingly poured into a g"

game_fit_scores.rationale: This live-action UGC archetype shows zero gameplay and references a different game ("Crossword Go"), so it does nothing to communicate Bounce Blast 3D's physics chain-reaction loop — which is the entire reason this game converts. The dancing-young-man hook also skews younger and more hype-driven than the calm 25-55 casual physics-puzzle audience, and the warm beige/blue UGC palette clashes with the moody dark-purple/amber game aesthetic. Only worth testing if rebuilt as a "satisfied older player + screen-recorded chain reaction B-roll" hybrid; as-is, do not greenlight.

Audience overlap is strong — the calm, satisfaction-led jigsaw hook targets the same 25-55 casual puzzle demographic Bounce Blast 3D wants. However, there are two real frictions: (1) the member palette is light/airy (greys, sky blue, green) which clashes hard with the game's dark moody purple + yellow-orange accent, so creatives would need a full recolor pass; (2) jigsaw drag-and-place only partially mirrors the core loop — tap-to-place tray onto grid is on-brand, but the hook completely misses the physics chain-reaction payoff that is the game's actual "satisfaction" beat. Recommend testing only if reshot with the game's dark palette AND extending past 3s into a ball-bounce chain reaction reveal.

The "POV: rushing home to play" UGC hook skews young Gen-Z TikTok, which is a poor fit for Bounce Blast 3D's 25-55 casual relaxer audience — they're looking for calm/satisfying vibes, not hyped bedroom-dive energy. Visually the member palette (purple/blue/yellow) is roughly compatible with the game's deep-purple + amber accent scheme, but the live-action hoodie/bedroom setting clashes hard with the 3D abstract no-character aesthetic. Mechanic-wise the concept name-drops a competitor block puzzle and never showcases the physics chain-reaction payoff that's actually this game's hook — only worth testing as a low-budget UGC variant if the back half is re-cut to feature satisfying ball-bounce gameplay and the brand reference is removed.

This is a Thief Puzzle / "pull-the-pin" style fail-bait archetype with stick-figure 2D animation, bright yellow/green palette, and a narrative-driven character gag — none of which align with Bounce Blast 3D's character-free, calm/satisfying 3D abstract physics-puzzle DNA (dark purple/orange palette, block placement + chain reactions). Mechanic match is near zero: there's no thief/draw-puzzle hook to graft onto tap-to-place block clearing without misrepresenting the product, which risks installs but terrible D1/D7 retention. Audience skews younger and meme-driven vs. the 25-55 relaxed casual target — do not greenlight; if testing fail-emotion creatives, build a native version using a "wrong block placement → physics fail → satisfying retry" beat instead.

Palette clash is severe: the archetype's pastel pink/baby blue kitchen aesthetic directly conflicts with Bounce Blast's dark moody purple + amber UI, so a literal port would feel off-brand. Mechanically, "pouring into a jar" is a weak analog for the game's tap-to-place + physics chain-reaction loop — there's no block placement, no bounce payoff, and no level-clear moment to anchor the satisfaction beat. Only worth pursuing as a loose inspiration: borrow the ASMR pour cadence and apply it to balls cascading into a grid, but don't commission this archetype as-is (also note n=1 advertiser, low velocity 0.21 — weak signal).

brief.title: Tap. Place. Bounce. — Chain-Reaction Satisfaction
brief.hook_3s: Close-up: a manicured thumb taps a glowing amber block from the tray and snaps it into a dark purple grid — the block pulses gold, then the whole row erupts into bouncing balls.
scene_flow: 0–3s HOOK: Tight overhead phone-in-hand shot. Thumb drags a #FDBA4D block from the tray onto a moody #20232B grid. Satisfying click + amber glow on placement.
3–7s BUILD: Camera pushes in. Player taps two more blocks, completing a row. Tension beat — the placed blocks shimmer and tremble.
7–11s PAYOFF: Blocks transform into glowing yellow-orange balls that ricochet across the board, smashing purple obstacles in a physics chain reaction. Slow-mo on the biggest combo.
11–14s ESCALATE: Quick montage of 3 harder levels clearing in cascading bounces, level meter fills, 'LEVEL 7' badge pops.
14–15s CTA: Game logo over a final satisfying clear. Download button pulses in #FDBA4D.
brief.visual_direction: Lean fully into the game's dark, moody DNA: deep charcoal #20232B background with rich #403F6D purple UI frames and tray, every interactive moment kissed with bright #FDBA4D yellow-orange light (block glow, ball trails, progress bar, CTA). 3D Casual Abstract style — soft global illumination, subtle bevels on blocks, glossy spherical balls with motion blur trails. No characters; the hero is the player's thumb plus the board. ASMR-grade SFX cues: soft tactile click on placement, low whoosh on transform, popcorn-pop chain on chain-reaction. Camera stays phone-POV/overhead until the payoff, where it tilts in for a 3D parallax reveal of bouncing physics.
brief.rationale: Keeps the winning satisfaction/drag-place hook that scores 75/100 on audience but fixes both flagged frictions: full recolor into the game's signature dark purple + amber palette, and an extended 15s arc that pays off the physics chain-reaction beat the jigsaw reference completely misses. Test against a control that cuts at the 7s placement beat to isolate whether the bounce payoff lifts IPM — our bet is the chain-reaction reveal is the true 'satisfaction' moment for this game and will outperform pure jigsaw mimicry.

brief.title: POV: My Nightly Unwind Ritual
brief.hook_3s: POV: it's 10pm — phone tilts up, one tap drops a glowing amber block, and the whole grid erupts into a cascade of bouncing balls.
scene_flow: 0-3s: Dim bedroom ambience implied via soft purple glow on a phone screen (no on-camera person). Phone shows the Bounce Blast 3D grid waiting; finger hovers. Overlay: 'POV: my 10pm wind-down.'
3-7s: Tap places a yellow-orange block into the tray slot. Obstacles transform into bouncing balls — first chain reaction begins with satisfying thuds and amber sparks. Overlay: 'one tap…'
7-11s: Camera pushes in on the grid as balls ricochet, clearing rows in a cascading combo. Progress bar fills in #FDBA4D amber. Overlay: 'chain reaction unlocked.'
11-14s: 'Level Complete' burst in deep purple frame; next harder level previews. Overlay: 'level 47 — still hooked.'
14-15s: End card: logo + CTA. Overlay: 'Tap to bounce.'
brief.visual_direction: Lean fully into the 3D Casual Abstract aesthetic — no live action, no characters. Frame the phone screen as the entire world: deep charcoal #20232B background with subtle bokeh, deep purple #403F6D UI frames and tray, and the hero amber #FDBA4D accent reserved for the tappable block, progress bar, and chain-reaction sparks. Soft rim lighting on 3D blocks, glossy bouncing balls with motion blur trails, ASMR-grade satisfying micro-animations. Calm, cinematic, late-night-lamp mood — the opposite of frantic UGC. Audio: muted tap clicks, soft ball thuds, gentle synth pad.
brief.rationale: We're keeping the archetype's high-performing 'POV' framing but swapping the Gen-Z bedroom-dive energy for a calm, late-night unwind ritual that matches Bounce Blast 3D's 25-55 relaxer audience. The back half is fully re-cut to showcase the physics chain-reaction payoff (the actual differentiator) instead of generic block-stacking, and we drop any competitor name. Test as a low-cost UGC-adjacent variant against pure-gameplay control; expect stronger IPM with women 30+ on Facebook/TikTok.

brief.title: No. 001 — Pour the Bounce
brief.hook_3s: A glowing amber ball pours like liquid into a dark grid — then BOUNCES.
scene_flow: 0-3s: Macro ASMR shot of a velvet-purple drawer sliding open to reveal a single glowing amber sphere, against a moody #20232B void.
3-7s: A stream of glossy amber balls pours from above into a deep-purple grid jar, each impact a soft satisfying 'tok' — balls settle, filling rows.
7-11s: A finger taps to place the final colored block; the grid ignites and balls cascade in a physics chain reaction, bouncing off walls and clearing obstacles in a golden shower.
11-14s: Wide shot — level COMPLETE banner, the jar/grid empties with a satisfying whoosh, progress bar fills to next level.
14-15s: Phone-in-hand product shot with CTA pulse.
brief.visual_direction: Lean into the game's existing dark/moody DNA: rich #20232B background, deep #403F6D purple frames acting as the 'jar', and #FDBA4D amber balls as the hero accent that does all the lighting work. Studio-grade 3D Casual Abstract render — soft rim lights, shallow DOF, glossy subsurface on the balls so they read like honey or molten gold. Borrow the archetype's slow ASMR pour cadence and macro framing, but swap pastel kitchen for premium 'puzzle in a jar' minimalism. Every impact triggers a subtle screen-shake and particle sparkle in the accent hue.
brief.rationale: We keep the winning ASMR pour-and-fill cadence but reskin it entirely in Bounce Blast's dark-purple + amber palette to fix the 22/100 visual fit and avoid the off-brand pastel kitchen. The pour becomes the literal block-placement → physics-cascade payoff, anchoring the satisfaction beat to the actual core loop so users who tap through see the same mechanic they were sold. Ship this as the hero variant for 25-55 casual puzzle audiences; A/B the 'No. 001' label vs. a pure gameplay cold-open to isolate which drives IPM.
